description:
If running the Trivial File Transfer Protocol (TFTP) service is necessary, it should be configured to change its root directory at startup. To do so, find the path for the tftp systemd service: 
$ sudo systemctl show tftp | grep FragmentPath=
FragmentPath=/etc/systemd/system/tftp.service
and ensure the ExecStart line on that file includes the -s option with a subdirectory: 
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/in.tftpd -s
rationale:
Using the <tt>-s</tt> option causes the TFTP service to only serve files from the
given directory. Serving files from an intentionally-specified directory
reduces the risk of sharing files which should remain private.
ocil_clause: 'the "ExecStart" line does not have a "-s" option, and a subdirectory is not assigned'
ocil:
Verify the TFTP daemon is configured to operate in secure mode. Check if a TFTP server is installed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf list --installed tftp-server

tftp-server.x86_64    5.2-35.el9.x86_64
If a TFTP server is not installed, this is Not Applicable.

If a TFTP server is installed, check for the server arguments with the following command: 
$ systemctl cat tftp | grep ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/in.tftpd -s
fixtext:
Configure the TFTP daemon to operate in secure mode.

1. Find the path for the systemd service

$ sudo systemctl show tftp | grep FragmentPath=
FragmentPath=/etc/systemd/system/tftp.service

2. Edit the ExecStart line on that file to add the -s option with a subdirectory.

ExecStart=/usr/sbin/in.tftpd -s <sub idref="var_tftpd_secure_directory" />
srg_requirement: If the Trivial File Transfer Protocol (TFTP) server is required, the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 TFTP daemon must be configured to operate in secure mode.

stig.srg_requirement: If the Trivial File Transfer Protocol (TFTP) server is required, Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 TFTP daemon must be configured to operate in secure mode.
stig.vuldiscussion: Restricting TFTP to a specific directory prevents remote users from copying, transferring, or overwriting system files. Using the "-s" option causes the TFTP service to only serve files from the given directory.
stig.checktext:
Verify the TFTP daemon is configured to operate in secure mode.

Check if a TFTP server is installed with the following command:

$ sudo dnf list --installed tftp-server

Example output:

tftp-server.x86_64          5.2-35.el9.x86_64

Note: If a TFTP server is not installed, this requirement is Not Applicable.

If a TFTP server is installed, check for the server arguments with the following command:

$ systemctl cat tftp | grep ExecStart
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/in.tftpd -s /var/lib/tftpboot

If the "ExecStart" line does not have a "-s" option, and a subdirectory is not assigned, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Configure the TFTP daemon to operate in secure mode.

1. Find the path for the systemd service.

$ sudo systemctl show tftp | grep FragmentPath=
FragmentPath=/etc/systemd/system/tftp.service

2. Edit the ExecStart line on that file to add the -s option with a subdirectory.

ExecStart=/usr/sbin/in.tftpd -s /var/lib/tftpboot

title: Ensure tftp Daemon Uses Secure Mode
